skill.name: tool-presets
skill.description: Reusable tool set presets for Claude Code agents that need consistent permissions across development, analysis, research, file operations, and orchestration workflows.
skill.summary: Standardizes Claude Code agent tool permissions with reusable presets.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer command-execution hits are false positives from Markdown backtick code spans, and the weak-crypto hits are text substring matches in descriptions. The skill still merits a medium warning because it standardizes presets that grant Bash, file write, web access, task delegation, and a full-stack all-tools option.

medium_findings.title: Broad Agent Tool Permissions
medium_findings.description: The skill does not execute commands itself, but it defines presets that include Bash and a full-stack option with all tools. Agents using these presets can run shell commands, so users should apply them only to trusted agent roles.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented presets explicitly include Bash and all tools, which confirms broad command capability. The risk depends on how consuming agents use the preset, so the confidence is high but not absolute.

medium_findings.title: Presets Grant File, Web, and Delegation Capabilities
medium_findings.description: The preset list includes Write, Edit, WebSearch, WebFetch, Task, and TodoWrite. These capabilities are legitimate for agent setup, but they increase impact if an untrusted or poorly scoped agent adopts them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capability list is direct evidence of file modification, network retrieval, and task delegation permissions. The skill is documentation-only, so this is a permission-design risk rather than malicious code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are Markdown inline code spans and examples naming tools or agent roles. No executable Ruby code or shell interpolation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown documents, and the backticks delimit inline code or fenced examples. No Ruby source, command substitution, or runtime execution path appears in these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Substring Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding points to description text, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or algorithm selection logic was found in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited lines contain natural-language description fields. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm implementation, or security-sensitive data handling on those lines.

content.value_statement: Agent tool choices can drift across similar Claude Code workflows. This skill gives reusable presets for development, analysis, research, file operations, and orchestration.
actual_capabilities: Lists standard tool presets with their included tool names.
Explains when to use development, file operations, analysis, research, and orchestration presets.
Provides example agent roles that match each preset.
Shows a frontmatter pattern for referencing tool presets in an agent.
Documents a full-stack option for agents that need comprehensive tool access.
limitations: Does not enforce permissions or configure tools automatically.
Does not include executable scripts, tests, or validation logic.
The full-stack preset is described but has no separate reference file.
Some presets include broad tools such as Bash, WebFetch, Write, Edit, and Task.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent Agent Roles
use_cases.description: An agent builder can choose a preset before defining a new Claude Code role.

use_cases.title: Review Tool Permission Scope
use_cases.description: An engineering lead can compare presets before approving agent permissions.

use_cases.title: Document Team Agent Standards
use_cases.description: A platform team can reference common tool sets in internal agent documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Basic Preset
prompt_templates.prompt: I am creating a Claude Code agent for documentation updates. Which tool preset should I use, and why?

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Presets
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the dev-tools and file-ops presets for a refactoring agent. Recommend the narrower option.

prompt_templates.title: Map Roles to Presets
prompt_templates.prompt: Map these agent roles to the best preset: test automator, technical researcher, code reviewer, and workflow coordinator.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Preset Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent plan against the tool-presets guidance. Flag any preset that grants more access than the role needs.

output_examples.input: Which preset fits a code review agent?
output_examples.output: Use the analysis preset. It supports reading, searching, and running diagnostic tools for review work.

output_examples.input: Which preset fits a research agent?
output_examples.output: Use the research preset. It includes local reading, report writing, web search, and web page fetching.

output_examples.input: What should I avoid for a narrow documentation agent?
output_examples.output: Avoid full-stack access. A file operations preset is usually narrower and easier to review.

best_practices: Choose the narrowest preset that supports the agent role.
Review Bash, WebFetch, Write, Edit, and Task access before deployment.
Document why each agent needs its selected preset.
anti_patterns: Using full-stack access for a narrow task agent.
Granting Bash to agents that only need file search and reading.
Treating preset names as enforcement instead of documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It documents tool presets, but some presets include Bash for agents that execute commands.

faq.question: Which preset is safest for file-only work?
faq.answer: Use file-ops when the agent needs file reading, writing, editing, grep, and glob without shell execution.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is written for Claude Code agent tool selection and frontmatter usage.

faq.question: Can Codex users apply the same guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. Codex users can use the preset descriptions to reason about similar tool permission groups.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it recommends presets with broad permissions such as Bash, web access, and file writes.

faq.question: Does the skill validate an agent configuration?
faq.answer: No. It provides reference guidance and does not check or enforce a configuration.
